title: Add agent execution hooks for pre/post delegation
source: Claude Code hooks system and CI/CD pipeline patterns for agent orchestration (2025-2026)
description: The system lacks extension points around agent delegation. Adding a hooks system (inspired by Claude Code's own hooks architecture) would allow: pre-delegation validation (lint checks, dependency verification), post-delegation processing (auto-review, metric collection), and failure handling (retry logic, escalation). This makes the delegation pipeline composable and extensible without modifying core scripts.
proposed_changes: Create `scripts/hooks.py` with a hook runner that loads hook definitions from `.cto/config.json` under a `hooks` key. Support these hook points: (1) `pre_delegate` — runs before agent execution (e.g., validate ticket has acceptance criteria, check no conflicting in-progress tickets); (2) `post_delegate` — runs after successful completion (e.g., auto-run tests, update metrics dashboard); (3) `on_failure` — runs on agent failure (e.g., retry with different model, notify via roro event); (4) `on_review` — runs during review phase. Each hook is a shell command or Python callable. In delegate.py, add hook invocations around the `delegate_to_agent()` call in `main()` (around line 925): call `run_hooks('pre_delegate', ticket, agent)` before execution and `run_hooks('post_delegate', ticket, agent, output)` after. Add hook config examples to `.cto/config.json`.
